content.body: Electromagnetic induction is one of the most important discoveries in physics. Michael Faraday (1831) discovered that moving a magnet near a wire coil generates electricity. This principle underlies all electrical generators — turbines that power our cities.

Faraday's Law:
An EMF (electromotive force) is induced in a conductor when:
• The conductor moves through a magnetic field
• The magnetic field through the conductor changes

The size of the induced EMF increases when:
• Speed of movement increases
• Field strength increases
• More turns on the coil
• Using a stronger magnet

Lenz's Law:
The induced current creates a magnetic field that OPPOSES the change that caused it.

Simple rule: the induced current always tries to prevent the change in flux.
This is consistent with conservation of energy — you must do work against this opposition.

content.title: AC Generator (Dynamo)
content.body:
A coil rotates in a magnetic field:
• As coil rotates, flux through it changes → EMF is induced
• Direction of induced current reverses every half turn → AC (alternating current)
• Frequency of AC = speed of rotation (Uganda/UK mains: 50 Hz)
• Slip rings and brushes connect rotating coil to external circuit

Output: sinusoidal wave (alternating between positive and negative)

content.title: Transformers
content.body:
A transformer changes voltage using electromagnetic induction:

Transformer equation:
Vs/Vp = Ns/Np

Vp = primary voltage, Vs = secondary voltage
Np = primary turns, Ns = secondary turns

Step-UP transformer: Ns > Np → increases voltage
Step-DOWN transformer: Ns < Np → decreases voltage

Ideal transformer: Vp×Ip = Vs×Is (power is conserved)

Application: National Grid uses step-up transformers (to reduce energy loss in transmission at high voltage, low current), then step-down to household voltage.

questions.question: Electromagnetic induction is used in:
questions.explanation: Generators convert kinetic energy to electrical energy using electromagnetic induction.

questions.question: The induced EMF increases if you:
options: Increase the number of coil turns
questions.answer: Increase the number of coil turns
questions.explanation: More turns in the coil means more conductors cutting field lines → greater induced EMF.

questions.question: Lenz's Law states that the induced current:
options: Adds to the change causing it
Has no relationship to the causing change
Opposes the change that caused it
questions.answer: Opposes the change that caused it
questions.explanation: Lenz's Law: the induced EMF creates a current that opposes the change in magnetic flux that caused it.

questions.question: An AC generator uses slip rings (not a split-ring commutator) to produce:
questions.explanation: Slip rings allow continuous rotation and the output alternates direction — producing AC.

questions.question: Transformer: Vp=240V, Np=1000, Ns=100. Find Vs.
A step-up transformer:
questions.explanation: Step-up: Ns>Np → voltage increases. Since power is conserved (IV=const), current decreases.

questions.question: Why does the National Grid transmit electricity at high voltage?
options: To reduce power losses in cables (P=I²R, less current = less loss)
questions.answer: To reduce power losses in cables (P=I²R, less current = less loss)
questions.explanation: P_lost = I²R. High voltage means low current (for same power), dramatically reducing energy lost as heat in transmission cables.

questions.question: Uganda's mains frequency is 50 Hz. This means current reverses direction:
questions.explanation: At 50 Hz, current completes 50 full cycles per second — reversing direction 100 times per second (twice per cycle).

questions.question: Transformer: Vp=12V, Ip=5A. Vs=120V (ideal). Find Is.
Faraday's discovery (1831) showed that:
options: Changing magnetic field induces EMF in a conductor
questions.answer: Changing magnetic field induces EMF in a conductor
questions.explanation: Faraday discovered electromagnetic induction — a changing magnetic flux through a conductor induces an EMF.
